task_completion.description: Navigate to the Business Units section in the Eramba application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eramba web interface is visible, and the active menu or page header clearly indicates the 'Business Units' module is open.

task_completion.description: Open the form to add a new Business Unit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal, popup, or page titled 'Add Business Unit', 'New Business Unit', or similar is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the required name for the Business Unit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'IT Security Operations' is typed into the 'Name' or 'Title' input field of the creation form.

task_completion.description: Enter the required description for the Business Unit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The description field contains text including the keywords 'security monitoring', 'incident response', and 'vulnerability management'.

task_completion.description: Save the new Business Unit and verify it appears in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The form is submitted, and 'IT Security Operations' is visible as a saved entry in the main Business Units list, grid, or a success confirmation message is displayed.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Eramba graphical user interface rather than using terminal commands or database scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the Eramba web application. No terminal windows, curl commands, or SQL prompts are used to insert the data.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Eramba application and not a text editor or mock interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shows the standard Eramba GRC layout, navigation menus, and styling within a standard web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element, Console, etc.) are not open or being used to alter the DOM to display the expected business unit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Business Unit name is exactly 'IT Security Operations'. The description must contain the keywords 'security monitoring', 'incident response', and 'vulnerability management'. The creation must occur within the Eramba GRC web interface.
